content.text: I'll start by reading the plan and understanding the project structure.

content.content:
1	{
2	  "phase": "plan",
3	  "decisions": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "d1",
6	      "decision": "Mock useSession at module level via vi.mock('@/features/session/context') to control isConfigured and session values per test",
7	      "based_on": ["q1"],
8	      "alternatives_rejected": ["Manipulating localStorage/SessionProvider state to drive configured vs unconfigured paths — more complex, less explicit control"],
9	      "reversibility": "easy"
10	    },
11	    {
12	      "id": "d2",
13	      "decision": "Mock useProbabilityEdit from '@/lib/query/hooks' directly to return controlled mutation state objects (isPending, isSuccess, isError, data, error)",
14	      "based_on": ["q2"],
15	      "alternatives_rejected": ["Mocking '@/lib/api/client' and testing through real react-query mutation lifecycle — harder to control deterministic mutation states per test case"],
16	      "reversibility": "easy"
17	    },
18	    {
19	      "id": "d3",
20	      "decision": "No polyfill or mock needed for crypto.randomUUID — available in Node.js test environment",
21	      "based_on": ["q3"],
22	      "alternatives_rejected": ["Adding a global mock or polyfill for crypto.randomUUID — unnecessary since Node 19+ includes it"],
23	      "reversibility": "easy"
24	    },
25	    {
26	      "id": "d4",
27	      "decision": "Use separate test cases for success message and error message rendering",
28	      "based_on": ["q4"],
29	      "alternatives_rejected": ["Combining success/error assertions into a single form submission test — less clear, harder to debug failures"],
30	      "reversibility": "easy"
31	    },
32	    {
33	      "id": "d5",
34	      "decision": "Use fireEvent.change for slider and select interactions, consistent with existing test patterns",
35	      "based_on": ["q5"],
36	      "alternatives_rejected": ["Using @testing-library/user-event — not installed, would add inconsistent dependency pattern"],
37	      "reversibility": "easy"
38	    },
39	    {
40	      "id": "d6",
41	      "decision": "Use renderWithProviders from helpers.tsx for rendering, since vi.mock overrides useSession regardless of SessionProvider in the tree",
42	      "based_on": ["q1"],
43	      "alternatives_rejected": ["Custom wrapper with just QueryClientProvider — works but diverges from established test patterns"],
44	      "reversibility": "easy"
45	    }
46	  ],
47	  "steps": [
48	    {
49	      "step": 1,
50	      "task": "Create frontend/src/__tests__/TradingPanel.test.tsx with vi.mock setup for '@/features/session/context' and '@/lib/query/hooks', mock data fixtures (market object with outcomes/marginals), and helper to configure mock return values per test",
51	      "depends_on": []
52	    },
53	    {
54	      "step": 2,
55	      "task": "Write test: 'shows setup message when session is not configured' — mock useSession returning isConfigured:false, assert 'Set your Account ID' text is rendered and form is not present",
56	      "depends_on": [1]
57	    },
58	    {
59	      "step": 3,
60	      "task": "Write test: 'renders trading form when session is configured' — mock useSession returning isConfigured:true with session data, assert form elements are present (outcome select with market outcomes, probability slider, submit button with 'Submit Edit')",
61	      "depends_on": [1]
62	    },
63	    {
64	      "step": 4,
65	      "task": "Write test: 'updates probability display when slider changes' — use fireEvent.change on range input, assert formatProbability output updates in the label",
66	      "depends_on": [1]
67	    },
68	    {
69	      "step": 5,
70	      "task": "Write test: 'calls mutation.mutate with correct payload on form submission' — submit the form, assert mutate was called with accountId, variableId, outcomeId, probability, and idempotencyKey",
71	      "depends_on": [1]
72	    },
73	    {
74	      "step": 6,
75	      "task": "Write test: 'disables submit button and shows Submitting text when mutation is pending' — mock useProbabilityEdit returning isPending:true, assert button is disabled and shows 'Submitting…'",
76	      "depends_on": [1]
77	    },
78	    {
79	      "step": 7,
80	      "task": "Write test: 'shows success message with orderId when mutation succeeds' — mock useProbabilityEdit returning isSuccess:true with data.order.orderId, assert 'Order accepted: {orderId}' is rendered",
81	      "depends_on": [1]
82	    },
83	    {
84	      "step": 8,
85	      "task": "Write test: 'shows error message when mutation fails' — mock useProbabilityEdit returning isError:true with error object, assert error message is rendered",
86	      "depends_on": [1]
87	    },
88	    {
89	      "step": 9,
90	      "task": "Run vitest on TradingPanel.test.tsx to verify all tests pass, fix any issues",
91	      "depends_on": [2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]
92	    }
93	  ],

1	[Task trading-tests-001] PHASE 3b: IMPLEMENT
2	Title: Add tests for TradingPanel component
3	
4	Read your plan at .fleet/tasks/trading-tests-001/plan.json.
5	
6	YOU MUST IMPLEMENT the plan completely. This is the critical execution phase.
7	
8	For EACH step in plan.json, you MUST:
9	1. Announce: "=== Step N: {task description} ==="
10	2. Execute the step — create/modify the actual source files
11	3. Verify it worked (check file exists, run code, test passes)
12	4. Announce: "=== Step N complete ===" before moving to next
13	
14	DO NOT STOP until ALL steps are executed and verified.
15	DO NOT just read the plan — you must CREATE the files it specifies.
16	
17	DEPENDENCIES: Fresh worktree — install before testing:
18	  - Node: pnpm install or npm install
19	  - Python: pip install -r requirements.txt
20	
21	After all steps are complete, write .fleet/tasks/trading-tests-001/implementation.json:
22	{
23	  "phase": "implement",
24	  "steps_completed": [
25	    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "files_created": ["..."], "files_modified": ["..."], "verified": true }
26	  ],
27	  "all_steps_done": true
28	}
29	
30	BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "execute: trading-tests-001"

file.content:
[Task trading-tests-001] PHASE 3b: IMPLEMENT
Title: Add tests for TradingPanel component

Read your plan at .fleet/tasks/trading-tests-001/plan.json.

YOU MUST IMPLEMENT the plan completely. This is the critical execution phase.

For EACH step in plan.json, you MUST:
1. Announce: "=== Step N: {task description} ==="
2. Execute the step — create/modify the actual source files
3. Verify it worked (check file exists, run code, test passes)
4. Announce: "=== Step N complete ===" before moving to next

DO NOT STOP until ALL steps are executed and verified.
DO NOT just read the plan — you must CREATE the files it specifies.

DEPENDENCIES: Fresh worktree — install before testing:
  - Node: pnpm install or npm install
  - Python: pip install -r requirements.txt

After all steps are complete, write .fleet/tasks/trading-tests-001/implementation.json:
{
  "phase": "implement",
  "steps_completed": [
    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "files_created": ["..."], "files_modified": ["..."], "verified": true }
  ],
  "all_steps_done": true
}

BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "execute: trading-tests-001"

1. The TradingPanel component (search for TradingPanel in frontend/src)

7. Any vitest config
